economy-and-jobs.summary: Champions deregulation, tax cuts, and pro-business policies as central to job creation and economic growth.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Tried to repeal the ACA but promised to protect Medicare; has shifted positions on healthcare repeatedly.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Signature issue: built border wall, enacted travel bans, mass deportation agenda, and hardline enforcement.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Opposes student debt forgiveness and federal education mandates; has pushed to eliminate the Department of Education.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Increased military spending significantly and rebuilt military readiness as a top priority.

climate-and-environment.summary: Withdrew from the Paris Climate Agreement, rolled back EPA regulations, and dismisses climate science concerns.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Signed the First Step Act but also champions tough-on-crime rhetoric and aggressive policing.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: America First approach prioritizing bilateral deals; pursued diplomacy with North Korea while confronting China and Iran.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Generally opposes tech regulation but has targeted social media companies he views as biased against conservatives.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Repeatedly pledged not to cut Social Security or Medicare, breaking with traditional Republican orthodoxy.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Strong Second Amendment advocate backed by the NRA; reversed his brief support for red flag laws.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Repeatedly called for major infrastructure investment though never passed a comprehensive bill in his first term.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports reducing regulations to lower housing costs but has not prioritized federal housing programs.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Drill baby drill mantra; maximized fossil fuel production, opened federal lands, and fast-tracked pipelines.

reproductive-rights.summary: Appointed Supreme Court justices who overturned Roe v. Wade but has avoided supporting a national abortion ban.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Banned transgender military service, rolled back Obama-era LGBTQ protections, and opposes the Equality Act.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and emphasizes harsh penalties for drug trafficking, especially fentanyl.

voting-rights.summary: Pushed voter ID requirements and election integrity measures; made unsubstantiated claims of widespread voter fraud.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Signed the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act slashing corporate and individual rates as his signature legislative achievement.

labor-and-unions.summary: Generally sides with business over unions but has courted blue-collar union workers with populist messaging.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Reauthorized FISA surveillance powers but criticized intelligence community surveillance after claiming he was spied on.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Imposed sweeping tariffs on China, steel, and aluminum; views tariffs as a primary tool of economic and foreign policy.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Populist economic vision focused on reviving manufacturing and Rust Belt communities rather than Wall Street.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Supports protecting Medicare and has expressed openness to government action on drug pricing.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Hardline on border security and legal immigration reduction; co-sponsored strict enforcement legislation in the Senate.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Opposes broad student debt cancellation but has acknowledged the burden of education costs on working families.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports strong military but skeptical of extended overseas commitments and nation-building.

climate-and-environment.summary: Opposes aggressive climate regulations and Green New Deal; prioritizes energy jobs over emissions targets.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Takes a tough-on-crime stance while acknowledging systemic issues in communities he grew up around.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Skeptical of interventionism and NATO commitments; opposed Ukraine aid and favors restraint in foreign policy.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: More open than most Republicans to Big Tech regulation and has criticized Silicon Valley's cultural power.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Strongly defends Social Security and Medicare as essential programs for the working class.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Strong Second Amendment supporter who shifted from moderate gun views to opposing most gun regulations.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Supports infrastructure investment particularly for Rust Belt and rural communities.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Has spoken about housing affordability as a real crisis affecting working-class Americans.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Strongly backs fossil fuel production and opposes the transition away from oil and gas.

reproductive-rights.summary: Supports strict abortion restrictions and has called for a federal minimum standard protecting unborn life.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Opposes the Equality Act and has taken conservative positions on transgender issues in schools and sports.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and focuses on combating the opioid and fentanyl crisis devastating Appalachia.

voting-rights.summary: Supports voter ID laws and has echoed concerns about election integrity and mail-in voting.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports tax cuts for working families but has broken with GOP orthodoxy by being open to taxing corporations more.

labor-and-unions.summary: Unusually pro-union for a Republican; walked the UAW picket line and supports workers organizing.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Has expressed skepticism of government surveillance and Big Tech data collection practices.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Strong protectionist who supports tariffs to defend American manufacturing and opposes free trade agreements.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Supports pro-growth tax policy and expanded child tax credits to help working families.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Voted to repeal the ACA and supports market-based healthcare reforms over government-run programs.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Co-authored the 2013 Gang of Eight bill but later shifted to border-security-first and enforcement priorities.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Opposes blanket student loan forgiveness but has proposed income-based repayment reforms.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Leading defense hawk who consistently pushes for increased military spending and modernization.

climate-and-environment.summary: Acknowledges climate change but opposes aggressive regulations like the Green New Deal as economically harmful.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Voted against the Respect for Marriage Act and opposes the Equality Act on religious liberty grounds.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and has focused on combating drug trafficking from Latin America.

voting-rights.summary: Opposed federal voting rights legislation and supports state-level voter ID and election security measures.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports targeted tariffs especially against China and has pushed to decouple from Chinese supply chains.

climate-and-environment.summary: Dismisses climate change activism and opposes environmental regulations that restrict energy production.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Generally opposes government regulation of technology and favors free-market innovation.
